---
title: "FunctionalClustering"
author: "Joey Frost-Xenopoulos - 0730136"
date: "2025-03-06"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
server_dir <- normalizePath(
  Sys.getenv("MAGNETO_SERVER_DIR", unset = "D:/SERVER/1902"),
  winslash = "/",
  mustWork = FALSE
)
output_dir <- normalizePath(
  Sys.getenv("MAGNETO_OUTPUT_DIR", unset = file.path(getwd(), "output")),
  winslash = "/",
  mustWork = FALSE
)
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}

base_dir <- server_dir
data_files <- list.files(base_dir, pattern = "\\.RDS$|\\.rds$", full.names = TRUE)
names_data_files <- sub("\\.RDS$|\\.rds$", "", basename(data_files))
names_data_files <- sub("\\.tif-(Digitized|FailToProcess)$", "", names_data_files)

data_files <- data_files[!names_data_files %in% c("AGC-D-19020321-19020323", 
                                                  "AGC-D-19020830-19020901", 
                                                  "AGC-D-19021029-19021031", 
                                                  "AGC-D-19021106-19021107")]

names_data_files <- names_data_files[!names_data_files %in% c("AGC-D-19020321-19020323", 
                                                  "AGC-D-19020830-19020901", 
                                                  "AGC-D-19021029-19021031", 
                                                  "AGC-D-19021106-19021107")]
```

# Midnight to Midnight code
# ADJUSTS SO EVERYTHING IS CENTERED AROUND 638
```{r}
time_vec <- c()
data_vec <- c()

for(i in 1:length(data_files))
{
  data <- readRDS(data_files[i]) # Reading the RDS
  
  if(!is.null(data$BotTimeSeq)){
    time_vec <- c(time_vec, data$BotTimeSeq)
  }
  
  if(!is.null(data$TopTimeSeq)){
    time_vec <- c(time_vec, data$TopTimeSeq)
  }
}

for(i in 1:length(data_files))
{
  data <- readRDS(data_files[i])

  if(i == 1){
    data_vec <- c()
    data_vec <- c(data_vec, data$BottomTraceMatrix)
    final_val <- tail(data$BottomTraceMatrix, 1)
    adj_val <- data$TopTraceMatrix[1] - final_val
    new_First <- data$TopTraceMatrix - adj_val
    data_vec <- c(data_vec, new_First)
  }

  if(i != 1){
    if(!is.null(data$BotTimeSeq)){
      adj_val <- data$BottomTraceMatrix[1] - 638
      new_Bot <- data$BottomTraceMatrix - adj_val
      data_vec <- c(data_vec, new_Bot)
    }
    
    if(!is.null(data$TopTimeSeq)){
      adj_val <- data$TopTraceMatrix[1] - 638
      new_Top <- data$TopTraceMatrix - adj_val
      data_vec <- c(data_vec, new_Top)
    }
  }
}

time_stops <- which(time_vec == 0)
mid_mid <- list()
source_vec <- c()

oop <- na.omit(time_vec)
time_stops <- which(oop == 0)


for(i in 1:(length(time_stops) - 1)) {
  
  # Exclude the ending zero by adjusting the endpoint
  time <- oop[time_stops[i]:(time_stops[i+1] - 1)]
  data <- data_vec[time_stops[i]:(time_stops[i+1] - 1)]
  mid_mid[[i]] <- data.frame(x = time, y = data)
}

mid_mid[[6]]$x <- sort(mid_mid[[6]]$x)
mid_mid[[77]]$x <- sort(mid_mid[[77]]$x)
mid_mid[[78]]$x <- sort(mid_mid[[78]]$x)


mid_mid[[length(time_stops)]] <- data.frame(x = time, y = data)
```

```{r}
library(splines)
library(mixture)
library(energy)
library(tclust)

knots <- seq(from = 0, to = 23, by = 1) # Defining the knots
coef <- matrix(0, 350, 28) # Coef Matrix
index <- 1 # For index of coef matrix

for(i in 1:350)
{
  # print(i)
  y <- mid_mid[[i]]$y
  x <- sort(mid_mid[[i]]$x)
  fit <- lm(y ~ bs((x), knots = knots, intercept = FALSE)) # Running bspline()
  coef[index,] <- as.vector(fit$coefficients)
  index <- index + 1 # Counting
}

nabool <- apply(coef,1,function(x)sum(is.na(x)))
goodcoef <- coef[which(nabool==0),]

clustres <- gpcm(goodcoef, G = 2:10) # Clustering, G number of groups
table(clustres$best_model$map)
```

# AVERAGE CLUSTER

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(mclust)
library(dplyr)

library(magneto)

Mres <- Mclust(goodcoef, G = 8)

x_grid <- seq(0, 24, length.out = 200)  # A smooth x-grid for plotting
basis_full <- bs(x_grid, knots = knots)        # Full basis (no intercept column yet)
interceptMat <- cbind(1, as.matrix(basis_full))  # Add intercept → 29 columns

# Drop last two columns to match your clustering input
interceptMat_trimmed <- interceptMat[, 1:27]
mat <- Mres$parameters$mean
funMeans <- interceptMat_trimmed %*% mat  # Mean curves for each cluster

# Create dataframe for plotting
plotdf <- data.frame(x = x_grid, funMeans)
colnames(plotdf) <- c("x", paste0("Cluster ", 1:ncol(funMeans)))

# Pivot longer for ggplot
plotdf_long <- pivot_longer(plotdf, cols = -x, names_to = "Cluster", values_to = "y")

# Plot using ggplot
ggplot(plotdf_long, aes(x = x, y = y, color = Cluster)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  labs(title = "Mean Functional Curves by Cluster",
       x = "x", y = "Pixel Height") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),             # Remove all grid lines
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),# Add axis lines
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)    # Center the title
  )

```


```{r}
# Get cluster assignments for each observation
cluster_assignments <- Mres$classification

# Identify which rows belong to Cluster 1
cluster1_indices <- which(cluster_assignments == 4)

# Compute functional curves for all Cluster 1 days
cluster1_curves <- interceptMat_trimmed %*% t(goodcoef[cluster1_indices, ])

# Create plotting dataframe for Cluster 1 days
cluster1_df <- data.frame(x = x_grid, cluster1_curves)
colnames(cluster1_df) <- c("x", paste0("Day_", cluster1_indices))

# Pivot longer
cluster1_long <- pivot_longer(cluster1_df, 
                             cols = -x, 
                             names_to = "Day", 
                             values_to = "y")

# Extract just Cluster 1 mean for plotting
cluster1_mean <- plotdf_long %>% 
  filter(Cluster == "Cluster 4")

# Create the plot
ggplot() +
  # Individual days as semi-transparent lines
  geom_line(data = cluster1_long, 
            aes(x = x, y = y, group = Day), 
            color = "#F08080", 
            alpha = 0.3,
            size = 0.5) +
  # Mean curve as prominent line
  geom_line(data = cluster1_mean, 
            aes(x = x, y = y), 
            color = "red", 
            size = 1.5) +
  labs(title = "Cluster 4: Mean Curve with Individual Days",
       x = "Hour of Day", 
       y = "Pixel Height") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )
```

```{r}
# Prepare data for all clusters
full_plot_data <- map_dfr(1:8, function(cluster_num) {
  cluster_indices <- which(cluster_assignments == cluster_num)
  cluster_curves <- all_curves[, cluster_indices, drop = FALSE]
  
  # Individual curves
  ind_df <- data.frame(
    x = x_grid,
    y = c(cluster_curves),
    Day = rep(paste0("Day_", cluster_indices), each = length(x_grid)),
    Cluster = paste("Cluster", cluster_num),
    Type = "Individual"
  )
  
  # Mean curve
  mean_df <- data.frame(
    x = x_grid,
    y = plotdf_long %>% 
        filter(Cluster == paste0("Cluster ", cluster_num)) %>% pull(y),
    Day = "Mean",
    Cluster = paste("Cluster", cluster_num),
    Type = "Mean"
  )
  
  bind_rows(ind_df, mean_df)
})

# Create faceted plot
ggplot(full_plot_data, aes(x = x, y = y, group = Day, color = Type)) +
  geom_line(data = filter(full_plot_data, Type == "Individual"),
            alpha = 0.2,
            size = 0.3) +
  geom_line(data = filter(full_plot_data, Type == "Mean"),
            size = 1.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Individual" = "gray50", "Mean" = "red")) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(500, 700)) +
  facet_wrap(~Cluster, ncol = 3) +
  labs(x = "Hour of Day", y = "Pixel Height") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90"),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
```





```{r, warning = FALSE}
cluster_assignments <- clustres$best_model$map

# Plotting each day's data with fitted functions
for(i in 1:nrow(goodcoef)) {
  
  #if(i == 80 || i == 129 || i == 261 || i == 265){next}

  plot((mid_mid[[i]]$x), mid_mid[[i]]$y, pch = 19, col = cluster_assignments[i],
       main = paste("Day", i, "- Cluster", cluster_assignments[i]),
       xlab = "Time", ylab = "Response", cex = 1, type = "p")
  
  fit <- lm(y ~ bs((x), knots = knots), data = mid_mid[[i]])
  
  xgrid <- seq(min(mid_mid[[i]]$x, na.rm = TRUE), 
               max(mid_mid[[i]]$x, na.rm = TRUE), 
               length.out = 400)
  
  points(xgrid, predict(fit, newdata = list(x = xgrid)),
         col = "blue", lwd = 2, type = "l")
  
  # abline(v = knots, lty = 2, col = "blue")
}

```




# PLOTTING ALL THE DATA

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
# Create a full year date sequence
start_date <- as.Date("1902-01-01")
end_date <- as.Date("1902-12-31")
all_dates <- seq(start_date, end_date, by = "day")
first_days_of_month <- all_dates[format(all_dates, "%d") == "01"]
first_days_of_month[first_days_of_month == "1902-04-01"] <- as.Date("1902-04-02")
first_days_of_month[first_days_of_month == "1902-05-01"] <- as.Date("1902-05-02")
first_days_of_month[first_days_of_month == "1902-08-01"] <- as.Date("1902-08-02")
first_days_of_month[first_days_of_month == "1902-11-01"] <- as.Date("1902-11-02")
first_days_of_month[first_days_of_month == "1902-12-01"] <- as.Date("1902-12-02")

# Initialize variables
all_data <- data.frame(x = numeric(), y = numeric(), date = as.Date(character()))
cumulative_x <- 0 
day_tracker <- c()

# For x labs
month_tick <- data.frame(month = "January", x = 0)

for(i in 1:length(data_files)) {
  data <- readRDS(data_files[i])
  day_1 <- as.Date(substr(data_files[i], 22, 29), format = "%Y%m%d")
  day_2 <- as.Date(substr(data_files[i], 31, 47), format = "%Y%m%d")
  
  # Check if there's a day in between day_1 and day_2
  if (as.numeric(difftime(day_2, day_1, units = "days")) > 1) {
    # If there's a day in between, store the 3 days
    day_in_between <- day_1 + 1  # The day in between (one day after day_1)
    day_tracker <- c(day_tracker, format(day_1, "%Y-%m-%d"), format(day_in_between, "%Y-%m-%d"), format(day_2, "%Y-%m-%d"))
  } else {
    # If there's no day in between, store just the two days
    day_tracker <- c(day_tracker, format(day_1, "%Y-%m-%d"), format(day_2, "%Y-%m-%d"))
  }
  
  if(day_1 %in% first_days_of_month)
  {
    month_name <- format(day_1, "%B")
    cat("The month is:", month_name, "\n")
    # Append Month
    month_tick <- rbind(month_tick, data.frame(month = month_name, x = cumulative_x))
  }
  
  if(!is.null(data$BottomTraceMatrix)) {
    adj_val <- data$BottomTraceMatrix[1] - 638
    new_Bot <- data$BottomTraceMatrix - adj_val

    all_data <- bind_rows(all_data, data.frame(
      x = cumulative_x + seq_along(data$BotTimeSeq),
      y = new_Bot,
      date = day_1)
    )
    cumulative_x <- max(all_data$x)
  }

  if(!is.null(data$TopTraceMatrix)) {
    adj_val <- data$TopTraceMatrix[1] - 638
    new_Top <- data$TopTraceMatrix - adj_val

    all_data <- bind_rows(all_data, data.frame(
      x = cumulative_x + seq_along(data$TopTimeSeq),
      y = new_Top,
      date = day_2)
    )
    cumulative_x <- max(all_data$x)
  }
}

# Ensure unique_days is in Date format
unique_days <- as.Date(unique(unique_days))
all_dates <- all_dates[-1]
all_data$Date <- as.Date(all_data$date, format = "%Y-%m-%d")
last_index <- max(which(all_data$date == as.Date("1902-11-10")))
# Create NA rows
na_rows <- as.data.frame(matrix(NA, nrow = 25000, ncol = ncol(all_data)))
colnames(na_rows) <- colnames(all_data)

# Fill 'x' column in na_rows with properly spaced dates
na_rows$x <- seq(all_data$x[last_index] + 1, 
                 by = diff(all_data$x[1:2]), 
                 length.out = 25000)
adj_data <- rbind(all_data[1:last_index, ], na_rows, all_data[(last_index + 1):nrow(all_data), ])

ggplot(adj_data, aes(x = x, y = y)) +
  geom_line() +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = month_tick$x,
    labels = month_tick$month
  ) +
  labs(x = "Month", y = "Magnetic Variation", title = "1902 Magnetic Variation Data") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +  # Base size scales text elements
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),  
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),  
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),  
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16),  # Larger title
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),  
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10)  
  )

ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "trace_data_plot.png"), 
       width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

```
